content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Cyclic Components". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/977/E
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, the number of nodes in the graph. The next n lines contain the adjacency list of the graph, where each line contains the node and its neighbors separated by spaces.
content.outputFormat: The number of cyclic components in the graph.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use Depth-First Search (DFS) to detect cycles in the graph and count the number of cyclic components.

code.Python3:
import sys
MAX = 100001
n = int(input())
adj = [[] for _ in range(n)]
visited = [0] * n
recStack = [0] * n
def dfs(node):
    visited[node] = 1
    recStack[node] = 1
    for neighbor in adj[node]:
        if visited[neighbor] == 0:
            dfs(neighbor)
        elif recStack[neighbor] == 1:
            # cycle detected
            pass
    recStack[node] = 0
for i in range(n):
    node = int(input().split()[0]) - 1
    neighbors = list(map(lambda x: int(x) - 1, input().split()[1:]))
    adj[node] = neighbors
count = 0
for i in range(n):
    if visited[i] == 0:
        dfs(i)
        count += 1
print(count)
